SourceProxy.label: SHYX Adaptive Isotropic Remesher
SourceProxy.name: SHYXAdaptiveIsotropicRemesher

Documentation.short_help: Curvature-aware CGAL remesh with min/max edge length.

SourceProxy.Documentation: Adaptive isotropic remeshing using CGAL Adaptive_sizing_field (discrete curvature). Edge lengths are limited by Min/Max Edge Length; AdaptiveTolerance controls fineness together with curvature. With a Selection (or Selection Cell Array Name), only those faces are remeshed; otherwise the whole surface is remeshed. Edges on the boundary between selected and unselected triangles are treated as CGAL feature edges for remesh and for optional smooth_shape vertex pinning (together with angle-based sharp edges). Post-remesh smooth_shape uses the same Protection Angle for sharp edges; with Feature Mask enabled it also evaluates the same mask array and threshold on the remeshed surface (after interpolation) to clip features and mark mask-region boundaries—no centroid classification. Port 2 is the input mask patch; port 3 is the threshold extract on remeshed port 0. Port 1 lines come from port 2 (input mask) when the mask is valid; otherwise from the full remeshed surface. Port 1 vertex cells (feature points) are emitted only when Shape Smooth Iterations > 0: from port 3 if the mask is on, otherwise from the full remeshed surface. Requires CGAL 6.0+.

InputProperty.name: Selection
InputProperty.label: Selection
InputProperty.Documentation: Optional vtkSelection (active selection). When present and non-empty, CGAL remeshing is applied only to the indicated faces (same convention as SHYX Delete Selected Cells). The boundary between selected and non-selected faces is also constrained as feature edges for remesh and smooth_shape (with ProtectAngle / Feature Mask rules).

StringVectorProperty.name: SelectionCellArrayName
StringVectorProperty.label: Selection Cell Array Name
StringVectorProperty.Documentation: If Selection is empty: name of a cell data array on Input. Faces are remeshed when value > 0.5 (float) or non-zero (integer types). The patch boundary vs unselected faces is also used as feature edges for remesh / smooth_shape (same as the Selection port).

DoubleVectorProperty.name: MinEdgeLength
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Min Edge Length
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Minimum edge length; must be > 0. ParaView BoundsDomain (scaled_extent 0.001) suggests 0.1% of the input axis-aligned bounding box longest side; use scale/Reset next to the property.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: MaxEdgeLength
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Max Edge Length

DoubleVectorProperty.name: AdaptiveTolerance
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Adaptive Tolerance (tol)
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: CGAL adaptive sizing tolerance; smaller values tend to refine more within min/max bounds.

IntVectorProperty.name: NumberOfRelaxationSteps
IntVectorProperty.label: Relaxation Steps
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: CGAL vertex relocation steps per remesh iteration (higher = smoother surface; 0 = no relaxation).

IntVectorProperty.name: ShapeSmoothingIterations
IntVectorProperty.label: Shape Smooth Iterations
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: Post-remesh CGAL smooth_shape iterations. 0 disables. Sharp-edge vertices (Protection Angle) stay fixed.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: ShapeSmoothingTimeStep
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Shape Smooth Time Step
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: CGAL smooth_shape time step (smoothing speed). Typical range about 1e-6 to 1. Used only when Shape Smooth Iterations > 0.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: ProtectAngle
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Protection Angle
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Feature edge angle threshold in degrees.

IntVectorProperty.name: SharpFeatureSideFilter
IntVectorProperty.label: Sharp side filter
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: Optional filter on interior sharp edges after CGAL detect_sharp_edges, using signed dihedral angle (approximate_dihedral_angle). Excludes concave (signed < 0) or convex (signed > 0) from remesh/shape constraints and from port 1. Boundary edges unchanged. If convex/concave looks swapped, flip mesh orientation or pick the other entry.

IntVectorProperty.name: RemeshProtectConstraints
IntVectorProperty.label: Remesh: Protect constraints
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: CGAL protect_constraints. When ON, feature edges are neither split nor collapsed. When OFF, constrained edges may be split or collapsed per CGAL (see collapse_constraints).

IntVectorProperty.name: RemeshCollapseConstraints
IntVectorProperty.label: Remesh: Collapse constraints
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: CGAL collapse_constraints. When Protect constraints is OFF, allows collapsing constrained edges. Ignored by CGAL when Protect constraints is ON.

IntVectorProperty.name: RemeshRelaxConstraints
IntVectorProperty.label: Remesh: Relax constraints
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: CGAL relax_constraints. When ON, endpoints of constrained and boundary edges may move along constrained polylines during relaxation.

IntVectorProperty.name: FeatureMaskEnabled
IntVectorProperty.label: Feature Mask
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: When on: threshold input faces to port 2; port 1 sharp features are computed only on that patch; on the full surface, sharp edges are intersected with the mask for CGAL remesh / smooth_shape. Choose a point- or cell-centered array, a threshold (tuple magnitude > threshold), and optional All Scalars for point arrays.

StringVectorProperty.name: FeatureMaskArrayName
StringVectorProperty.label: Array
StringVectorProperty.Documentation: Name of a point- or cell-centered array. If both exist with the same name, cell data is used. Tuple magnitude must be > Threshold for a cell (or per-point corner) to pass.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: FeatureMaskThreshold
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Threshold
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Cells pass when tuple magnitude is strictly greater than this value (scalar or vector magnitude). Typical: 0 to keep values > 0.

IntVectorProperty.name: FeatureMaskAllScalars
IntVectorProperty.label: All Scalars
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: When the chosen array is point-centered: if on, every corner of a cell must pass the magnitude test; if off, one passing corner is enough (vtkThreshold-style). Ignored when the array resolves to cell data.

IntVectorProperty.name: UseUpdateAttributes
IntVectorProperty.label: Interpolate attributes
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: If ON, point/cell data are interpolated onto the new mesh.
